summary.summary: Adds a new Quick-Pick Boluses feature (user-facing) and related UI, data, settings, and project changes. The home-screen "+" tab bar icon gains a long-press (0.5s) action that queries manual bolus history from CoreData (last 90 days) and scores amounts using a LoopFollow-derived algorithm (time-of-day Gaussian sigma = 60 min, weekday/weekend weighting, 10-day recency half-life). Up to five highest-scoring amounts are suggested; suggestions are deduplicated and filtered by a minimum score. Selecting a suggestion requires biometric authentication (Face/Touch ID) before handing off to the existing apsManager bolus pipeline. The feature includes a slide-to-enact confirmation UI, a help/info sheet, an enableQuickBolus toggle in settings (persisted and added to settings export), formatter and localization updates, and multiple UI/safety fixes (disable interactive dismissal while enacting, guards to prevent double-fire, auth-failure handling, haptic feedback). Implementation touches HomeStateModel (loadQuickBolusSuggestions, enactQuickBolus), TrioSettings (enableQuickBolus), Formatters (leading zero fix), project file additions, and several new view files for the Quick-Pick Boluses UI.
summary.userFacingImpact: New Quick-Pick Boluses are available via long-pressing the + button on the home screen; the sheet shows suggested bolus amounts derived from your manual bolus history or an explanatory "no history" alert if not enough data exists. Picking a suggested amount then requires on-device authentication and confirmation before a real bolus is enacted. The feature can be disabled via a new "Enable Quick Bolus" setting (persisted). Because this change introduces a new, direct bolus entry path, human review is recommended for safety verification before release.
